description: Hand-labeled gold set for eval storage quality. Each session lists correct observations (extracted by reduce), false reinforcements, and missing observations the system should have captured.

sessions.summary: User pasted PR URL, asked to verify only indentation changes + a label addition. Claude ran diffs, confirmed.

correct.title: Autonomous multi-step investigation without check-ins
Verify-before-concluding: implementation may already exist

missing: Passes PR URLs as the full problem statement — bare URL + one-line instruction is a complete task

sessions.summary: User loaded plugin creation workflow, Claude tried to scaffold from memory, plugin didn't exist on disk. User said 'Delete all your memories.'

correct.title: Commands are terse and assume full context
Nuclear reset: wipes memory state without explanation or ceremony

missing: Stale memories that confidently assert false state trigger immediate purge, not correction — the causal link between wrong memory and nuclear reset

sessions.summary: User asked which Sidekiq DogStatsD metrics are histograms, worried about cardinality. Claude investigated through gem source code internals.

correct.title: Autonomous multi-step investigation without check-ins
Cardinality is a first-class infrastructure concern

false_reinforcements.title: Dependency hygiene is a first-class concern
false_reinforcements.reason: Session was about metrics cardinality, not dependency cleanup

false_reinforcements.title: Tests the full pipeline end-to-end before activating anything
false_reinforcements.reason: No pipeline testing occurred in this session

missing: Reads library source code (gem internals, middleware) when docs don't answer the question
Asks infrastructure cost questions proactively before shipping, not reactively after problems

sessions.summary: User said 'resolve [PR URL]'. Claude found 2 approvals, no open comments. User corrected: 'There is a merge conflict.' Claude investigated, rebased in worktree, did semantic merge, force-pushed.

correct.title: Autonomous multi-step investigation without check-ins
Worktree + .minions directory structure for ticket work
Commands are terse and assume full context
Rebase over merge — always, even mid-conflict
Conflict resolution requires semantic understanding, not mechanical take-one-side
Force-push is the expected close to a rebase — no confirmation needed

false_reinforcements.title: Unattended execution mode: no check-ins, no confirmations
false_reinforcements.reason: She interacted mid-session to correct Claude about the merge conflict — not unattended

missing: Corrects by stating facts, not giving instructions — 'There is a merge conflict' not 'fix the merge conflict'
Uses abstract verbs that delegate scope determination — 'resolve' means figure out everything needed to merge
Expects cross-PR awareness when resolving conflicts — Claude needed to understand a parallel PR's rewrite to resolve

sessions.summary: Fetched Claude Code docs, asked for PR creation script, caught quoting bug in Claude's output, abandoned script ('forget this part'), tried to trigger heredoc hook.

correct.title: Commands are terse and assume full context
Interrupts mid-execution to redirect, then expects seamless continuation
Cut-and-abandon: drops half-built work without ceremony when it's not worth finishing
Stdin over positional args for multiline content

false_reinforcements.title: Autonomous multi-step investigation without check-ins
false_reinforcements.reason: Session was interactive, not an autonomous investigation chain

false_reinforcements.title: Kills agents that go off-track rather than waiting them out
false_reinforcements.reason: She abandoned a script, not an agent

missing: Audits Claude's code output in real-time and catches bugs before Claude does — 'will the body not cause the same issue'
sessions.labeling_notes: #83 title 'cut-and-abandon' reads too harsh — better framed as 'decisive about what's not worth finishing'

sessions.summary: Split PR #151 into independently deployable slices. Cherry-picked files, hit test failures, debugged regression from source branch, fixed consumer breakage, got 610 tests green, created draft PRs.

correct.title: Commands are terse and assume full context
Interrupts mid-execution to redirect
Split PRs by independent deployability, not just logical grouping
Interrupted mid-execution with a factual correction, expects seamless pivot
Self-observation: verify tests pass on origin/main before assuming a failure is mine
Self-observation: cherry-picking files from a branch can silently import regressions

false_reinforcements.title: Unattended execution mode
false_reinforcements.reason: She interrupted to correct 'compare to origin/main latest' — interactive, not unattended

missing: When splitting PRs, expect consumer breakage and be prepared to fix callers not in the original diff
UNDER-EXTRACTED: 9 observations from 484 messages is too few — scan truncation (12K cap) destroyed most of the session before reduce saw it
sessions.labeling_notes: This session exposed a pipeline bug: scan's 12K char cap treats 484-message sessions the same as 8-message sessions. Fixed in b6d1b21.

meta.recall_notes: 10 missing observations across 6 sessions. True recall unmeasurable without exhaustive labeling, but directionally: the system misses causal links, communication patterns, and meta-observations about the user's relationship with Claude's output.
